commands.chain_as_tree: HIPENGINE_HIP_ARCH=gfx1151 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt python3 scripts/dflash_chain_e2e_bench.py --backend hip_gfx1151 --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --verifier-mode native_bulk_bplus1 --full-attn-chain-mode batched --tree-mode chain_as_tree --hardware-gpu 'AMD RYZEN AI MAX+ 395 w/ Radeon 8060S' --max-prompts 1 --decode-tokens 8 --draft-budgets 1,2,4,8 --json /tmp/hipengine-chainastree-speedgate.json
commands.chain_batched: HIPENGINE_HIP_ARCH=gfx1151 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt python3 scripts/dflash_chain_e2e_bench.py --backend hip_gfx1151 --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build --verifier-mode native_bulk_bplus1 --full-attn-chain-mode batched --hardware-gpu 'AMD RYZEN AI MAX+ 395 w/ Radeon 8060S' --max-prompts 1 --decode-tokens 8 --draft-budgets 1,2,4,8 --json /tmp/hipengine-batched-speedgate.json
commands.chain_c1_loop: HIPENGINE_HIP_ARCH=gfx1151 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt python3 scripts/dflash_chain_e2e_bench.py --backend hip_gfx1151 --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build --verifier-mode native_bulk_bplus1 --full-attn-chain-mode c1_loop --hardware-gpu 'AMD RYZEN AI MAX+ 395 w/ Radeon 8060S' --max-prompts 1 --decode-tokens 8 --draft-budgets 1,2,4,8 --json /tmp/hipengine-c1loop-speedgate.json
commands.serial: HIPENGINE_HIP_ARCH=gfx1151 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt python3 scripts/dflash_chain_e2e_bench.py --backend hip_gfx1151 --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build --verifier-mode serial_in_place_single_slot --hardware-gpu 'AMD RYZEN AI MAX+ 395 w/ Radeon 8060S' --max-prompts 1 --decode-tokens 8 --draft-budgets 1,2,4,8 --json /tmp/hipengine-serial-speedgate.json

decision_reason: DDTree verifier path lands as drop-in equivalent to chain batched on the degenerate (linear) tree topology used here (chain_as_tree = parent_rows -1,0,1,...,B-1).  At every B={1,2,4,8} the tree verifier produces bit-equal AR-matching tokens, GPU accept matches CPU oracle, and accepted-token counts match chain exactly.  Per-cycle verify time is within 6% of chain batched in either direction (FASTER at B=1 and B=4 by ~5%, equal at B=2, ~2% slower at B=8).  The tree-aware GQA gate kernel's per-row ancestor-mask check adds NO meaningful overhead vs the chain kernel.  Still 2.0-5.0x slower than serial c=1 across all B, same as chain batched.  The DDTree infrastructure is correct and competitive; an actual speed win over serial requires a real branching tree drafter (top-K expansion) which is task #13's full form.

hardware.gpu: AMD RYZEN AI MAX+ 395 w/ Radeon 8060S

task: DDTree verifier integration vs chain DFlash: tree verifier on degenerate (linear) tree topology compared against chain batched, chain c1_loop, and serial baselines at B={1,2,4,8}.
title: DDTree verifier (chain_as_tree topology) vs chain DFlash speed-gate
